maml:description.maml:para: Connects to IntelligenceX (native or app-server) and returns a client instance.

Use Native for ChatGPT OAuth login (no local Codex binary). Use AppServer to talk to a locally running Codex app-server process. The cmdlet sets the active default client, so subsequent cmdlets can omit -Client.
Transport to use (Native or AppServer). Native uses ChatGPT OAuth directly.
Possible values: Native, AppServer, CompatibleHttp, CopilotCli
Path to the codex executable. Defaults to 'codex' on PATH.
Arguments to pass to the app-server. Defaults to 'app-server'.
Working directory for the app-server process.
Enable diagnostics output (RPC calls, login events, stderr).
Ignore .intelligencex/config.json overrides.
Optional ChatGPT account id to use when multiple auth bundles are present.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Native transport (ChatGPT OAuth)
App-server transport with explicit executable path
App-server transport with custom working directory and args
Enable diagnostics output
Pin a specific OpenAI account id when multiple bundles are present

maml:description.maml:para: Disconnects the active IntelligenceX client and clears local session context.
Releases app-server/native client resources, clears diagnostics subscriptions, and resets default thread/initialization state. If no client is active, the cmdlet exits without error.
Client instance to disconnect. Defaults to the active client.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Disconnect the active client
Disconnect a specific client object
Clean up at the end of a script

maml:description.maml:para: Initializes the client handshake with the app-server.
Sends client identity metadata (name, title, version) to app-server. Some flows require initialization before login, chat, or review operations.
Client instance to initialize. Defaults to the active client.
Client identifier sent to the app-server (machine-friendly).
Client display title sent to the app-server (human-friendly).
Client version sent to the app-server for telemetry/capability context.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Initialize the app-server session
Initialize a specific client instance
Initialize immediately after connect in a script bootstrap

maml:description.maml:para: Returns the current account details.
Returns the account identity for the active session, such as email and account id. Useful to confirm which credential bundle is currently active.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of typed account info.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Get account info
Get raw JSON account payload
Verify account after login in a script

maml:description.maml:para: Authenticates the active client with an OpenAI API key.
Stores the API key in the active client for API-based requests. Use only if ChatGPT OAuth is not desired or available.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
OpenAI API key used for authentication.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Log in with an API key
Log in with an explicit client and verify account access
Use API key auth in non-interactive CI flows

maml:description.maml:para: Super-easy chat command that handles connect, init, login, thread, and send.

Convenience entry point for quick chat: it connects, logs in (if needed), creates or reuses a thread, sends input, and returns the resulting turn. Supports streaming output and a simple DSL for text/image inputs.
Message text to send.
Pipeline input for DSL lines (text, image, url).
Parse Text as a simple DSL (lines starting with image:, url:, text:).
Model identifier. Defaults to gpt-5.3-codex.
Login method: ChatGpt, ApiKey, or None.
API key to use when Login is ApiKey.
Open the login URL in the default browser.
Write streaming deltas to the host.
Wait N seconds for streaming output after sending.
Reset thread before sending.
Client name for initialization.
Client title for initialization.
Client version for initialization.
Codex executable path.
Codex app-server arguments.
Working directory for app-server.
Workspace directory for file writes (sets sandbox policy).
Allow network access when using a workspace sandbox.
Approval policy (for example auto).
Image file path to include with the message.
Image URL to include with the message.
System instructions for the assistant.
Reasoning effort level.
Possible values: Minimal, Low, Medium, High, XHigh
Reasoning summary level.
Possible values: Auto, Concise, Detailed, Off
Response verbosity level.
Possible values: Low, Medium, High
Sampling temperature.
Save image outputs to the specified directory.
Download image URLs when saving images.
Overwrite existing files when saving images.
Prefix for saved image file names.
Return raw JSON response.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Quick chat
Chat with streaming output

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 3 ----------
command:example.dev:code: @" text: Describe the image image: C:\temp\diagram.png "@ | Invoke-IntelligenceXChat -Dsl

dev:remarks.maml:para: Use the DSL to mix text and an image
Use API key login
Run with a workspace sandbox and network access

maml:description.maml:para: Starts the ChatGPT login flow and returns the authorization URL.
Opens the browser to complete the ChatGPT OAuth flow. Pair with Wait-IntelligenceXLogin to poll for completion and store the resulting credentials.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of typed login data.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Start login and open the browser
Return the raw JSON response for custom handling
Start login and explicitly wait for completion

maml:description.maml:para: Lists collaboration modes available in the current app-server runtime.
Use this cmdlet to discover valid collaboration mode values before setting them in configuration or passing them to review/chat workflows.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of typed mode models.

dev:remarks.maml:para: List collaboration modes
Show mode names and mapped mode values
Return raw JSON response

maml:description.maml:para: Executes a command through the app-server.
Runs a process via the app-server with optional sandbox settings and timeout.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Command and arguments.
Working directory for the command.
Sandbox type for execution.
Enable network access for sandboxed runs.
Writable root paths for sandboxed runs.
Command timeout in milliseconds.
Return raw JSON response.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Run a command in the repository root
Run a command in a sandboxed workspace
Run a command with a timeout

maml:description.maml:para: Reads the effective app-server configuration with layer metadata.
Returns merged config values plus metadata that explains where values come from (for example defaults, workspace files, or environment overrides).
App-server client instance to query. Defaults to the active client.
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of typed config models.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Read effective config as structured output
Inspect where a specific setting comes from

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 3 ----------
command:example.dev:code: Get-IntelligenceXConfig | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Layers | Select-Object Version, DisabledReason

dev:remarks.maml:para: Inspect active configuration layers
Read raw JSON for custom handling

maml:description.maml:para: Writes multiple app-server configuration values in one request.
Sends a batch of key/value updates. This is useful for related settings you want to apply together. Hashtable values are converted to JSON before sending.
App-server client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Hashtable of configuration key/value pairs to write.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Set multiple values at once
Include booleans and nested objects
Apply a batch, then inspect the effective config

maml:description.maml:para: Reads server-defined constraints for supported configuration values.
Returns allowed values for key settings (for example approval policy and sandbox mode) so scripts can validate config before writing changes.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of typed requirements models.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Get config requirements
Get raw JSON output
Inspect allowed approval policies

maml:description.maml:para: Writes a single app-server configuration value.
Updates one config key on the app-server. PowerShell values are converted to JSON before sending. Use Get-IntelligenceXConfig to verify the effective result and layer origin.
App-server client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Configuration key to write.
Configuration value. Converted to JSON (string, number, boolean, array, object).

dev:remarks.maml:para: Set the model used by default
Set a boolean or numeric value
Write a nested object using a hashtable

maml:description.maml:para: Installs GitHub Copilot CLI using a selected install strategy.
Executes the platform-specific installer command and optionally returns command metadata. Supports WhatIf/Confirm through ShouldProcess.
Install method to use (Auto, Winget, Brew, Apt, etc. depending on platform).
Possible values: Auto, Winget, Homebrew, Npm, Script
Installs a prerelease build when available.
Returns the resolved install command object after successful execution.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Install Copilot CLI using the auto method
Preview installation without executing changes
Install prerelease and return command metadata

maml:description.maml:para: Shows platform-specific installation commands for GitHub Copilot CLI.
This cmdlet does not install anything. It returns suggested install command metadata so you can preview, log, or execute it manually.
Returns commands for installing prerelease builds.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Show stable install commands
Show prerelease install commands
Inspect command and arguments for automation scripts

maml:description.maml:para: Watches JSON-RPC notifications from the app-server.
Streams notification events until cancelled. Use method filters to observe specific protocol events such as turn progress, login completion, or status changes.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Optional JSON-RPC method filter list. Matching is case-insensitive.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Watch all events
Watch only turn deltas
Watch multiple event types

maml:description.maml:para: Uploads textual feedback to the app-server feedback endpoint.
Use this cmdlet to send reproduction notes, quality feedback, or analyzer observations collected during reviews and local runs.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Feedback text content to upload.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1 ----------
command:example.dev:code: Send-IntelligenceXFeedback -Content "The review missed the nullable warning."

dev:remarks.maml:para: Send feedback

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2 ----------
command:example.dev:code: $note = "Reviewer suggestion: add timeout handling.`nObserved on Windows PS 7.5."; Send-IntelligenceXFeedback -Content $note

dev:remarks.maml:para: Send feedback from a multi-line here-string

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 3 ----------
command:example.dev:code: Send-IntelligenceXFeedback -Client $client -Content "MCP OAuth instructions were unclear for first-time users."

dev:remarks.maml:para: Send feedback with an explicit client

maml:description.maml:para: Runs health checks for OpenAI app-server and optional Copilot CLI.
Returns health status for the active IntelligenceX client and, optionally, a Copilot CLI instance using explicit or config-derived options.
OpenAI/app-server client instance. Defaults to the active client.
Run a Copilot CLI health check.
Ignore .intelligencex/config.json overrides.
Copilot CLI path.
Copilot CLI URL (host:port).
Copilot CLI working directory.
Auto-install Copilot CLI if missing.
Copilot auto-install method to use when -CopilotAutoInstall is set.
Possible values: Auto, Winget, Homebrew, Npm, Script
Copilot auto-install prerelease.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Check OpenAI app-server health
Check OpenAI and Copilot CLI health
Check Copilot with explicit path and auto-install enabled

maml:description.maml:para: Lists threads currently loaded in the app-server process.
Returns thread ids that are currently active in memory for the running app-server session. Useful for diagnostics and cleanup scripts.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of typed thread-id models.

dev:remarks.maml:para: List loaded threads
Get raw JSON output
Expand loaded thread ids as plain values

maml:description.maml:para: Waits for the login flow to complete.
Polls the app-server for login completion. Use after Start-IntelligenceXChatGptLogin.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Optional login identifier to wait for.
Maximum wait time in seconds before cancellation.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Wait for the current login to finish
Wait for a specific login with a shorter timeout
Start and wait in one flow

maml:description.maml:para: Starts an MCP OAuth login flow and returns the browser authorization URL.
Use this cmdlet when an MCP server reports OAuth auth status. The response includes a LoginId and AuthUrl you can open in a browser.
App-server client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
MCP server identifier to start login for.
MCP server name to start login for.
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of a typed model.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Start OAuth login by server name and open the browser
Start OAuth login by server id
Discover an OAuth server first, then start login
Return raw JSON response for custom handling

maml:description.maml:para: Reloads MCP server configuration in the running app-server.
Refreshes MCP configuration after file changes so newly added servers, tools, and auth settings are visible without restarting the app-server process.
App-server client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Reload MCP server config after editing config files

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2 ----------
command:example.dev:code: Invoke-IntelligenceXMcpServerConfigReload; Get-IntelligenceXMcpServerStatus | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Servers | Select-Object Name, AuthStatus

dev:remarks.maml:para: Reload config, then verify with server status
Reload config for an explicit client instance

maml:description.maml:para: Lists configured MCP servers with auth, tool, and resource status.
Queries the app-server MCP registry and returns one page of server status data. The result includes Servers (current page) and NextCursor (for pagination).
App-server client instance to query. Defaults to the active client.
Pagination cursor from a previous response (NextCursor).
Maximum number of server entries to return in this page.
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of typed models.

dev:remarks.maml:para: List the first page of MCP server statuses
Inspect server names, auth status, and available tool counts
Continue with a pagination cursor
Return raw JSON when you need provider-specific fields

maml:description.maml:para: Sends a message to a thread and starts a turn.
Queues a user message on an existing thread. Returns the created turn so you can poll for output with Get-IntelligenceXTurnOutput.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Thread identifier to send the message to.
Message text to send.
Optional model override.
Working directory to pass to the app-server.
Approval policy name to use.
Sandbox type (for example 'workspace' or 'danger-full-access').
Enable network access for sandboxed runs.
Writable root paths for sandboxed runs.
Return raw JSON response.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Send a message in an existing thread
Send a message with a temporary model override
Send a message with a workspace sandbox
Return raw JSON output

maml:description.maml:para: Lists models available for the current transport context.
Returns model metadata for the active client. Raw JSON mode is available only on app-server transport because native transport returns strongly typed models directly.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Returns raw JSON-RPC payload (app-server transport only).

dev:remarks.maml:para: List available models
List models as raw JSON (app-server transport only)
Filter to model ids for selection prompts

maml:description.maml:para: Starts a review flow for a thread.
Triggers the app-server review pipeline for a thread and target. Use TargetType to choose what to review (uncommitted changes, a branch, a commit, or custom text).
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Thread identifier.
Delivery mode (for example immediate).
Target type: uncommittedChanges, baseBranch, commit, custom.
Target value (branch name, commit SHA, or custom text).
Return raw JSON response.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Review uncommitted changes
Review a specific commit
Review a base branch
Return raw JSON output

maml:description.maml:para: Invokes a raw JSON-RPC method directly against app-server.
Low-level escape hatch for advanced scenarios not covered by high-level cmdlets. Parameters are converted from PowerShell objects/hashtables to JSON payloads.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
JSON-RPC method name (for example thread/list).
Optional method parameters supplied as a PowerShell object/hashtable.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Call a raw RPC method
Read config using raw JSON-RPC
Call an RPC method with nested parameters

maml:description.maml:para: Lists available skills.
Scans for skills in the provided working directories and returns the resolved list.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Working directories to scan for skills.
Force reload of skills.
Return raw JSON response.

dev:remarks.maml:para: List skills in the current workspace
Force reload skills
Return raw JSON output

maml:description.maml:para: Enables or disables a skill entry in app-server skill configuration.
Updates the persisted skill config for a specific skill path so future tool runs include or exclude that skill.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Path to the skill configuration entry.
Set to $true to enable, $false to disable.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Disable a skill by config path
Enable a skill again
Enable multiple known skill config files in a loop

maml:description.maml:para: Archives an existing thread so it no longer appears in active workflows.
Use this cmdlet to hide completed or obsolete conversations from normal thread listings while preserving history. The archive operation is non-destructive and can be used for housekeeping.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Identifier of the thread to archive.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Archive a thread
Archive a thread after a completed review
Archive using an explicit client instance

maml:description.maml:para: Lists available threads.
Returns paged threads with optional filtering by model provider.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Cursor from a previous list response.
Maximum number of threads to return.
Sort key (created_at or updated_at).
Filter by model providers.
Return raw JSON response.

dev:remarks.maml:para: List recent threads
Continue a previous page
Filter by model provider

maml:description.maml:para: Rolls back the last N turns from a thread.
Removes recent turns from a thread so you can re-run, correct, or branch from an earlier conversation state.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Identifier of the thread to modify.
Number of most recent turns to remove.
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of typed thread info.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Rollback the last two turns
Rollback one turn and resend an adjusted message
Return raw JSON response

maml:description.maml:para: Resumes an existing thread so new messages can be sent to it.
Loads thread context back into the active app-server session and returns thread metadata. Useful after reconnecting or when switching between multiple threads.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Identifier of the thread to resume.
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of typed thread info.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Resume a thread by id
Resume and send a follow-up message
Return raw JSON response

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new conversation thread.
Starts a fresh thread on the app-server with a selected model and optional execution settings like sandbox and approval policy.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Model identifier to use (for example gpt-5.3-codex).
Working directory to pass to the app-server.
Approval policy name to use.
Sandbox mode to use (for example 'workspace' or 'danger-full-access').
Return raw JSON response.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Start a thread with the default model
Start a thread scoped to a repo and sandboxed
Start a thread with an approval policy

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new thread fork from an existing thread's history.
Use this when you want to branch a conversation without mutating the original thread. The fork inherits prior context and gets a new thread id.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Identifier of the source thread to fork.
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of typed thread info.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Fork a thread
Fork and continue conversation in the new thread
Return raw JSON response

maml:description.maml:para: Interrupts a running turn for a thread.
Requests cancellation for an in-progress turn. Use this for long-running responses, accidental prompts, or when you need to restart execution with different instructions.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Identifier of the thread that owns the running turn.
Identifier of the turn to interrupt.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Stop a running turn
Stop the turn returned by Send-IntelligenceXMessage
Interrupt with an explicit client

maml:description.maml:para: Extracts outputs from a turn.
Filters and saves outputs from a completed turn, including images and text.
Turn to read outputs from.
Return only image outputs.
Return only text outputs.
Return only the first N outputs.
Save image outputs to the specified directory.
Download image URLs when saving images.
Overwrite existing files when saving images.
Prefix for saved image file names.
Output original TurnOutput objects when saving images.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Get all outputs
Save image outputs to disk
Return only the first text output

maml:description.maml:para: Requests user input through the app-server.
Prompts for one to three questions and returns collected answers in order. Useful for interactive script checkpoints that need explicit user confirmation or values.
Client instance to use. Defaults to the active client.
Questions to ask (minimum 1, maximum 3).
Returns the raw JSON-RPC payload instead of typed response data.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Ask for two inputs
Return raw JSON response
Use responses in script flow
